message:
Build MergeExecutionOptions for merge_ordered with struct update syntax

`merge_ordered` was constructing its `MergeExecutionOptions` by creating
a `..default()` value and then mutating the `sort` field in place. That
pattern is flagged by recent clippy lints (field-reassign-with-default)
and is noisier than necessary for a three-line setup.

Rewrite as a single struct expression:

    let options = MergeExecutionOptions {
        sort: true,
        ..MergeExecutionOptions::default()
    };

Behavior is identical — `sort: true` still overrides the default and
every other field inherits from `Default` — but the binding can now be
immutable and the intent (force sorted output for ordered merges) is
visible at a glance.

No other call site touched; this is a purely local refactor inside the
public `merge_ordered` helper.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
